summary: Justin Brennan teaches the fundamentals of buying multifamily apartments (5+ units) as a value-add investment strategy. He covers deal sourcing through brokers, basic financial analysis using NOI and cap rates, and deal structuring with minimal out-of-pocket capital by raising investor money. While comprehensive, the content is foundational education rather than specific to Brian's 15-50 unit value-add focus in GA/TN/AL.
takeaways: Access 60% pre-market off-market deals by building broker relationships on 10-30 unit on-market deals before scaling to 50+
Target 14-20%+ IRR, 6-10%+ cash-on-cash, 1.8x+ equity multiple as deal thresholds
NOI formula: Gross Potential Income minus economic loss (12-18% currently) plus other income minus property expenses = Net Operating Income
Cap rate = NOI / Property Value; every $1,000/month NOI increase = ~$220,000 value increase at 5.5% cap rate
Raise 90% equity from investors by bringing 10% yourself, getting 2-3% acquisition fee that returns most capital at close
For first deal, keep deal size 10-30 units to access easier financing, broker relationships, and mom-and-pop sellers

Make money 5-8 ways: acquisition fees (2-3%), asset management (1-2% revenue), 30-40% cash flow/profits, tax benefits (bonus depreciation), construction fees (5-8%), disposition fees (0.5-2%), and double-dipping as GP investor

skill_candidates.change: Build relationships with 3+ brokers on on-market 10-30 unit deals to access their 60% pre-market inventory
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin shows how touring 3 properties with different KC brokers led to pre-market deal access; states 'you will never see these pre-market deals without some relationships'

skill_candidates.change: Quickly screen deals using IRR 14-20%+, CoC 6-10%+, EM 1.8x+ thresholds before deeper underwriting
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin emphasizes these 'big metrics you're looking at when you're underwriting these deals' and states these metrics determine if deals 'attract investors'

skill_candidates.change: Apply NOI formula (GPI - economic loss + other income - expenses) and cap rate valuation (Value = NOI / cap rate) to every deal
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin teaches 'the power of NOI' and demonstrates $1,000 monthly NOI increase = $220k value lift at 5.5% cap rate, framing it as foundational to 'get that net operating income as high as humanly possible'

skill_candidates.change: Structure first deal with 10% GP capital, 90% LP investor capital using acquisition fees to return most GP down payment at closing
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin breaks down a 17-unit deal where $42k GP investment generates $37.5k acquisition fee at close, leaving sponsor only $4.5k out-of-pocket while controlling the deal

title: Real Estate Investing 101: How To Buy Your First Multifamily Apartment In 90 Days - Complete Guide
